zh.Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可維其哈布盧可
zh.Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United K: 英國英格蘭素福爾可維其哈布盧可
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott是Walter de Pygott II和Unknown的女兒，於大約1258年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可維其哈布盧可。她於1280年與William De Colpton III在英國英格蘭素福爾可維其哈布盧可結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1330年在英國英格蘭肯特享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott是Walter de Pygott II和Unknown的女儿，于大约1258年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可維其哈布盧可。她于1280年与William De Colpton III在英国英格兰素福爾可維其哈布盧可结婚。他们至少是8个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1330年在英国英格兰肯特享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Pygott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Pickett
meanings: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Picot, Pigot, a pet form of Pic (see Pike 6). In Middle English, the form Piket (Old French Picquet) was also common.
sometimes a late development of   Pickard   1 or 2.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott was born about 1258, in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Walter de Pygott II and Unknown. She married William De Colpton III in 1280, in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. She died about 1330, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott est née environ 1258, à Wickhambrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Walter de Pygott II et Unknown. Elle a épousé William De Colpton III en 1280, à Wickhambrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1330, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott wurde um 1258 in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von Walter de Pygott II und Unknown geboren. Sie hat 1280 in Wickhambrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom William De Colpton III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Sie starb um 1330 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott è nata il circa 1258 nel Wickhambrook, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di Walter de Pygott II e Unknown. Lei sposato William De Colpton III nel 1280, nel Wickhambrook, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli. Morì circa 1330, nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygottは約1258年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウィックハンブルークでWalter de Pygott IIとUnknownの娘として生まれた。1280年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウィックハンブルークでWilliam De Colpton IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1330年にイギリスイギリスケントで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott(은)는 대략 1258년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 윅함브룩에서 Walter de Pygott II(와)과 Unknown의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 윅함브룩에서 William De Colpton III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1330년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1330, em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Pygott родилась около 1258 г. в местности виккхамбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Walter de Pygott II и Unknown. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William De Colpton III в 1280 г. в местности виккхамбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Она умерла около 1330 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет.
